_README: Manual topic injection. Usage: python -m pipeline.hermes_orchestrator --inject-topic drafts/EXAMPLE_toys_r_us.json   (add --no-validate to bypass the SearXNG demand gate). Required fields: company_name, founder_or_protagonist, year_anchor, story_kind, hq_country, hero, conflict. Optional top-level pins: archetype, narrator, visual_style — set any of these to force the cooldown engine's choice; remove them entirely to let S01 pick via rolling-window rotation. Any other keys (including this _README) flow through to incident.json unchanged and are visible to downstream stages.
one_line_pitch: How a $6.6bn leveraged buyout buried the world's biggest toy store right as Amazon redefined Christmas.
hero: Charles Lazarus, the WWII vet who turned a baby furniture store on Wisconsin Avenue into a 1,600-location category killer — then watched private equity hollow it out from the cheap seats.
conflict: A $6.6bn LBO by Bain, KKR, and Vornado that loaded Toys R Us with $5bn in debt right as Amazon and Walmart redefined toy retail, leaving the company unable to renovate stores or invest in e-commerce while servicing $400m a year in interest payments.
why_this_one: The 2017 Chapter 11 filing is well-documented in PACER (court records open), the LBO terms are in the SEC S-1 from the 2010 attempted IPO that the PE owners pulled, and ProPublica + The Atlantic covered the post-mortem in 2018-2019. Differentiation from the typical 'Amazon killed retail' angle: this is fundamentally a story about debt servicing, not consumer demand — Toys R Us sales were FLAT, not declining, when the company filed.
demonetization_risk_notes: Charles Lazarus is deceased (2018); LBO partners (Bain, KKR, Vornado) are public corporations and fair-comment territory. No living named villain. Brand IP (Geoffrey the Giraffe) referenced descriptively only.
